results.possibleIdentity: Scotty Snyder (born 1912) was a versatile artist and performer active in the New York and New Jersey avant-garde scenes from the 1960s through the 1980s. Originally known as a painter based in Summit, New Jersey, she exhibited her work at the Visual Arts Center of New Jersey (1962) and the Silvermine Guild of Artists (1969). Snyder is most notably recognized for her collaborations within the experimental theater and performance art communities. She was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) during the late 1960s, appearing in documentation and exhibition records related to the organization between 1968 and 1969. As a performer, she was a frequent collaborator with Robert Wilson, appearing in landmark productions such as 'The King of Spain' (1969) and 'The Life and Times of Sigmund Freud' (1970). She also appeared in Richard Foreman's 1981 film 'Strong Medicine' and was a member of the company for the Mabou Mines production of 'Dead End Kids: A History of Nuclear Power' (1980). Additionally, she was a close associate and subject for artist Stuart Sherman, who featured her in his performance films, including 'Scotty Snyder (All Around the Table)' (1987).
